page: Amazon Web Services c7g.medium is an arm64-based compute-optimized instance powered by a dedicated AWS Graviton3 processor using the ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 architecture. This server features 1 physical core and 1 vCPU running at 2.6 GHz, paired with 2.0 GB of system memory. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It does not include any local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1505.29 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1380.0. However, because the instance is limited to a single vCPU, multi-core performance is poor, matching the single-core metrics. Memory performance is also constrained, with a read-only large block bandwidth of 28.44 GB/sec and a write large block bandwidth of 10.44 GB/sec. Cryptographic operations are supported via hardware AES acceleration, but the architecture lacks advanced vector or matrix extensions like AVX or AMX.

Given its single-core profile and 2.0 GB of RAM, the c7g.medium is designed for lightweight, single-threaded workloads. It is suitable for basic web serving, simple text processing, and lightweight background tasks. It is not suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, or memory-intensive tasks. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a low-cost entry point into the AWS Graviton ARM ecosystem, providing dedicated CPU resources without the shared-core limitations of burstable instances, though resource density remains low.
description: Amazon Web Services c7g.medium is an arm64 compute-optimized instance powered by a dedicated 2.6 GHz AWS Graviton3 Neoverse-V1 processor. It features 1 physical core, 1 vCPU, and 2.0 GB of system memory, with no local storage or GPU hardware. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmarks show average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1380.0, but multi-core and memory bandwidth results are poor due to the single-vCPU limitation. The processor supports hardware AES encryption but lacks advanced vector extensions. This instance is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic web serving, and simple text processing tasks that benefit from dedicated ARM resources at a low entry cost.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.medium is a compute-optimized arm64 instance featuring 1 vCPU, 2.0 GB of RAM, and a 2.6 GHz AWS Graviton3 processor, suitable for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.medium is an arm64 compute-optimized instance with 1 vCPU, 2.0 GB RAM, and a 2.6 GHz AWS Graviton3 processor.
tagline: An entry level single core ARM processor instance designed for lightweight single threaded workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources without shared core overhead.
bullet_points: Dedicated AWS Graviton3 ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz
1 vCPU and 2.0 GB of system memory with no local storage
Average single-core performance but limited multi-core capabilities
Baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for lightweight web serving and single-threaded background tasks
